subject: UX & Research
title: The Power of Microcopy
description: Learn The Power of Microcopy in one minute.

hook.text: Google changed a button from 'Book a room' to 'Check availability.' Clicks rose 17%. Two words changed everything.

buildup.text: Microcopy is the tiny text on buttons, forms, and tooltips. It's small in size but huge in impact.

discovery.text: 'Submit' is vague. 'Send message' is clear. 'Get your free report' is motivating. Each word shapes user behavior.

twist.text: Most teams design the layout first and write the words last. But the words are what users actually read and act on.

climax.text: Write the microcopy first, then design around it. The words define the experience more than the pixels.

punchline.text: Small words, big impact. Microcopy is the most undervalued design tool.

quiz.question: Why did changing 'Book a room' to 'Check availability' increase clicks?
options: Lower commitment language reduced user hesitation about taking the action
The new button was a different color
